spec_match.rationale: Right column uses native Chrome 149 behaviour (real `text-overflow: ellipsis` inputs); left column simulates the pre-149 stickiness via JS that holds the ellipsis on focus. Three paired field types make the contrast legible.

interactive.rationale: Real focusable inputs, live focused/blurred state badges, three categories — visitor types and tabs through the comparison themselves.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises side-by-side old vs new for URL, email, and search; that's exactly what the page renders.

edge_cases.rationale: The comparison includes URL, email, search, password, readonly, contenteditable, RTL, and explicit End/Arrow caret instrumentation with live scroll/readout state.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colours and the comparison arrows are inline SVG icons instead of text glyphs.

summary: Strong side-by-side comparison with RTL and keyboard-caret coverage. Chrome DevTools MCP in Headless Chrome Canary 151 verified inline SVG arrows, End/Arrow caret status, all inputs named, and a clean console; the feature conformance route showed 9 pass / 0 fail.
